posts.question: Why does my dog tilt its head when I talk?
posts.answer: It helps them pinpoint your voice and read your tone. A head tilt means they're locked in on you.

posts.question: How do I stop my dog from pulling on the leash?
posts.answer: Stop walking the moment they pull, reward a loose leash. Pulling never moves them forward.

posts.question: What human foods are dangerous for dogs?
posts.answer: Chocolate, grapes, raisins, onions, garlic, and xylitol can be toxic. Keep them well out of reach.

posts.question: How many treats are too many?
posts.answer: Keep treats under 10% of daily calories. The rest should come from balanced meals.

posts.question: Why does my cat sleep in such weird positions?
posts.answer: Totally normal. Cats are flexible and only contort like that when they feel completely safe.

posts.question: What's the best gift for a pet lover?
posts.answer: Something personal. A portrait of their pet beats another mug. 65% of these are bought as gifts for a reason.

posts.question: What does it mean when my cat slow-blinks at me?
posts.answer: A slow blink is a cat's way of saying 'I trust you.' Blink slowly back to say it in return.

posts.question: Why does my dog follow me everywhere?
posts.answer: You're their safe place and their pack. Velcro behavior is usually deep attachment, not anxiety.

posts.question: Why does my dog lick me so much?
posts.answer: Licking is how dogs show affection, ask for attention, and taste the salt on your skin. It's love.

posts.question: Why does my cat knead me with its paws?
posts.answer: Kneading is a leftover kitten comfort habit. When they do it on you, they feel safe and content.

posts.question: Why does my cat get the zoomies at night?
posts.answer: Cats are wired to hunt at dawn and dusk. Nighttime sprints burn off that built-up prey energy.

posts.question: Why does my cat bring me 'gifts'?
posts.answer: Your cat sees you as family and shares the hunt. It's a strange but real sign of trust.

posts.question: Why does my dog eat grass?
posts.answer: Usually normal: boredom, taste, or fiber. See a vet only if it comes with frequent vomiting.

posts.question: How often should my pet see the vet?
posts.answer: Healthy adults: once a year. Puppies, kittens, and seniors: every 6 months to catch issues early.

posts.question: Why does my dog keep licking its paws?
posts.answer: Occasional licking is grooming. Constant licking can mean allergies, dry skin, or anxiety. Watch it.

posts.question: Do I really need to brush my dog's teeth?
posts.answer: Yes. Dental disease is one of the most common pet health issues. A few brushes a week makes a difference.

posts.question: How do I stop my puppy from biting?
posts.answer: Yelp 'ouch,' stop play for a moment, then redirect to a toy. Consistency teaches a soft mouth fast.

posts.question: How do I get my dog to love the crate?
posts.answer: Feed meals inside, toss treats in, never use it as punishment. Make the crate the best room in the house.

posts.question: How do I teach a reliable recall?
posts.answer: Make coming to you the best thing ever: high-value treats, happy voice, never call them for something bad.

posts.question: Should I let my new puppy roam the house?
posts.answer: Not at first. A small, safe space prevents accidents and builds good habits before you give freedom.

posts.question: How much should I feed my dog?
posts.answer: Start with the bag's guide for their goal weight, then adjust by body shape. You should feel ribs, not see them.

posts.question: Wet food or dry food for my cat?
posts.answer: Both work. Wet food adds hydration, dry is convenient. Many owners mix them. Ask your vet for the ideal ratio.

posts.question: Can dogs eat peanut butter?
posts.answer: Yes, in small amounts, but check the label first. If it lists xylitol, it's toxic. Avoid those brands.

posts.question: Is it normal for my dog to sleep all day?
posts.answer: Yes. Adult dogs sleep 12 to 14 hours, puppies and seniors even more. Watch for sudden changes, not the naps.

posts.question: Why does my dog eat poop and how do I stop it?
posts.answer: Common and usually harmless behavior. Pick up promptly, keep them busy, and ask a vet to rule out diet gaps.

posts.question: Why does my dog scoot its bottom on the floor?
posts.answer: Often full anal glands, sometimes worms or itch. If it keeps up, a quick vet visit sorts it out.

posts.question: Do pets remember us when we're gone?
posts.answer: Yes. Pets form deep bonds and clearly recognize the people they love, even after long time apart.

posts.question: What's a meaningful way to honor a pet who passed?
posts.answer: Many families keep a custom portrait so their companion stays a part of the home. A gentle, lasting keepsake.

posts.question: How do I take a great photo of my pet?
posts.answer: Natural light, get down to their eye level, and use a treat to catch their attention just above the lens.
